paper.title_en: Second-harmonic signal in electric-field-modulated EPR spectra of Fe3 spin triangles
paper.abstract_en: We present electric-field-modulated electron paramagnetic resonance (EFM-EPR) measurements on centrosymmetric single crystals of the molecular spin triangle $\mathrm{[{Fe_3}O({O_2}CPh){_6}(py){_3}]ClO{_4}{\cdot}py}$ ($\bf{Fe_3}$). We provide the first observation of second harmonic EFM-EPR signal in polynuclear magnetic molecules. This signal is simulated and explained in terms of an electric-field induced modulation of the isotropic exchange in the molecule, and of their symmetry lowering resulting from a Jahn-Teller effect. Additionally, an unexpected first harmonic EFM-EPR signal is observed. Various plausible symmetry-breaking mechanisms are discussed in an attempt to explain this feature, whose observation is unexpected in a nominally centrosymmetric crystal.
